name: The Puerta de Carmona and the Arco de la Rosa — circular by Marchena
name_original: La Puerta de Carmona y el Arco de la Rosa — circular por Marchena
region_intro: Hiking around Marchena offers routes through the Sevillian countryside, characterized by extensive olive groves, farmland, and undulating cereal fields. The region features the Vía Verde de la Campiña, a rehabilitated railway line ideal for exploring the landscape. Natural settings like the Ribera del Corbones provide additional outdoor opportunities. This area combines natural beauty with historical context, offering diverse terrain for hikers. Best hiking trails around Marchena The most popular hiking route is The Puerta de Carmona and the Arco de la Rosa — circular by Marchena, a 4.4 miles (7.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 50 minutes to complete. This easy route explores historic town features and immediate surroundings. Another top favourite among local hikers is Marchena – Marchena loop from Marchena, an easy 3.8 miles (6.1 km) path. This loop showcases the local landscape around Marchena. Local hikers also love the Charco Charcal Waterfall loop from Las Mezquitillas, an 8.7 miles (14.0 km) trail leading through natural areas with a waterfall, often completed in about 3 hours 53 minutes. Hiking around Marchena is defined by extensive olive groves, undulating cereal fields, and the Vía Verde de la Campiña. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to moderate excursions. The routes in Marchena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.9 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 50 hikers have used komoot to explore Marchena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Marchena, and what are their difficulty levels?
A: Marchena offers 12 hiking trails, catering to various fitness levels. You'll find 7 easy routes, 4 moderate options, and 1 more challenging trail, ensuring there's something for everyone.

Q: Are there any trails that feature the Vía Verde de la Campiña?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3900506" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Section of the Vía Verde Between Fuentes de Andalucía and Marchena&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent feature in the region. This rehabilitated railway line offers a peaceful route through extensive olive groves and farmland, ideal for exploring the Sevillian countryside.

Q: Can I find any trails with waterfalls near Marchena?
A: Yes, for those seeking a waterfall, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19321950" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco Charcal Waterfall loop from Las Mezquitillas&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.7-mile (14.0 km) trail that leads through natural areas to a scenic waterfall.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking around Marchena?
A: The landscapes around Marchena are characterized by the picturesque Sevillian countryside, featuring extensive olive groves, undulating cereal fields, and traditional farmland. The Ribera del Corbones also offers natural settings for outdoor enjoyment.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest to explore on the trails?
A: While hiking, you can appreciate the region's historical context. Some routes, like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010823354/the-puerta-de-carmona-and-the-arco-de-la-rosa-circular-by-marchena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Puerta de Carmona and the Arco de la Rosa — circular by Marchena&lt;/a&gt;, explore historic town features. You can also find highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5355917" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sevilla Gate&lt;/a&gt; and the nearby town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4863918" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuentes de Andalucía&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available in Marchena?
A: Yes, many routes around Marchena are circular. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010823354/the-puerta-de-carmona-and-the-arco-de-la-rosa-circular-by-marchena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Puerta de Carmona and the Arco de la Rosa — circular by Marchena&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14588574" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marchena – Marchena loop from Marchena&lt;/a&gt;, which are both easy loops showcasing the local landscape.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Marchena?
A: The trails in Marchena are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.9 stars from more than 10 reviews. Hikers often praise the serene Andalusian environment and the variety of terrain.

Q: Is Marchena a good destination for family-friendly hikes?
A: Yes, Marchena offers several easy trails suitable for families. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010823354/the-puerta-de-carmona-and-the-arco-de-la-rosa-circular-by-marchena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Puerta de Carmona and the Arco de la Rosa — circular by Marchena&lt;/a&gt; are relatively short and explore the town's surroundings, making them enjoyable for all ages.

Q: When is the best time of year to go hiking in Marchena?
A: The Sevillian countryside can be very warm in summer. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for hiking, allowing you to comfortably enjoy the olive groves and open fields. Winter can also be a good option for cooler weather.

Q: Are there opportunities for birdwatching while hiking?
A: Yes, for nature lovers and birdwatchers, the nearby Lantejuela Endorheic Complex Nature Reserve is a significant ecological site where visitors can observe flamingos and other migratory birds, adding a unique dimension to your outdoor experience.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of castles or other ancient structures?
A: You can find routes that provide glimpses of historical architecture. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27625420" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Marchenilla Castle loop from Fuentes de Andalucía&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate trail that offers views of the historic Marchenilla Castle.

Q: What is the typical duration for an easy hike in Marchena?
A: Easy hikes around Marchena typically range from 1.5 to 2 hours. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010823354/the-puerta-de-carmona-and-the-arco-de-la-rosa-circular-by-marchena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Puerta de Carmona and the Arco de la Rosa — circular by Marchena&lt;/a&gt; takes about 1 hour 50 minutes to complete.
tips: At the end of the Vía Verde de la Campiña, you reach the town of Marchena, where you can see from afar the two towers of the town that rise above the town itself.

It is one of the most enigmatic and least studied doors of the walled enclosure. Through it, the enclosure was directly accessed, while the fortress was reached, connecting the fortress with the field. Its function must have been that of a betrayal door or an escape door that communicated directly with the outside.
Beautiful door that is in the old center of the town of Marchena and that must have been part of the old wall that was in the town.
